# Figure description: Gamma IV.jpg

**Source**: `36572_Smolianova_1984/geophysical sections/Gamma IV.jpg`  
**Generated by**: claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## Figure Type

This is a **geophysical cross-section / profile** (профиль), combining a geological-geophysical interpretation panel at the top with gravity and/or magnetic anomaly curves below. It is a vertical section typical of Soviet-era regional geophysical survey reports, showing both interpreted subsurface geology and measured geophysical field data along a survey line.

---

## Coverage and Subject

The figure covers **Profile No. 1** (ПРОФИЛЬ 1̄ or №1), as labeled in the lower portion of the image. It appears to be associated with the regional survey of northern Kazakhstan (map sheets M-42 and/or N-42, 1:200,000 scale). The horizontal axis spans what appears to be a traverse of several hundred kilometers, with distance markers visible along the profile line. The vertical axis on the right side shows values in the range of approximately **−1400 to +200** (likely in milligals for Bouguer anomaly or nanoTeslas for magnetics, or relative units). Reference points labeled along the baseline include what appear to be station/picket markers and possibly borehole intersection points (marked with small circles — ○ symbols — at several locations along the profile).

---

## Key Content

**Upper panel (geological-geophysical interpretation strip):**
- A narrow stratigraphic/structural interpretation band runs across the top of the figure, divided into several vertical columns or blocks separated by vertical lines — likely representing different geological blocks or fault-bounded segments.
- Within each block, annotations indicate **rock densities** (in g/cm³, e.g., values such as **0.00,5/сМ³**, **0.00,1/сМ³**, **2.00,5/сМ³**, **2.00,1/сМ³**, and similar values) associated with interpreted lithological units.
- Layer boundaries and rock unit contacts are shown as horizontal and diagonal lines, suggesting **layered stratigraphy with faulting or folding**.
- Several vertical faults or contacts divide the section into distinct crustal blocks.

**Lower panel (geophysical anomaly curves):**
- Two curves are plotted:
  - A **relatively flat reference/zero line** running horizontally across the middle.
  - A **large-amplitude anomaly curve** with dramatic relief, showing:
    - A broad **positive anomaly (peak)** in the central portion of the profile, rising to approximately **+200 to +300 units**, forming a broad dome or arch shape.
    - A pronounced **negative anomaly (trough)** toward the right side of the profile, descending steeply to approximately **−1200 to −1400 units**, representing one of the most significant features of the section.
    - A moderate **negative departure** in the left portion (depths reaching perhaps −200 to −400 units).
- Small **circle symbols (○)** are plotted at several points along the profile, likely indicating **borehole intersections** or **control points** where ground-truth data constrain the geophysical model.
- The right-hand vertical scale is clearly graduated, with tick marks at regular intervals; values visible include **−1400, −1200, −1000, −800, −600, −400, −200, 0, +200** (approximate readings, in relevant geophysical units).
- A dashed vertical line near the right side of the profile appears to mark a significant **structural boundary or fault zone**, coinciding with the steep gradient of the negative anomaly.

## Text and Labels

Visible text (transcribed as closely as possible given image orientation — note the image appears to be **printed upside down or scanned inverted** in places):

  - `К отчету Западно-Сибирской партии за 1983-1984гг.` (To the report of the West Siberian party for 1983–1984)
  - `Условные обозначения на листе №3` (Legend on sheet No. 3)
- **Stamp/library mark (lower left):** A rectangular archival stamp, partially legible, reading something like:
  - `№ 36572` (report number)
  - `фонд геологической` (geological fund)
  - `информации` (information)
  - `Гос СГР` or similar (State geological archive designation)
- **Sheet/figure number:** `15` (circled, lower left corner) — likely plate or figure number 15
- **Bottom right:** `Лист №` followed by a number (sheet number reference)
- **Density annotations in upper panel** (repeated in multiple blocks, in g/cm³):
  - `0,00/сМ³`, `0,00,5/сМ³`, `2,00/сМ³`, `2,00,5/сМ³`, `2,00,1/сМ³`, `Рк,₁`, `Рк,₂`, `Рк,₃` (possibly formation or layer codes)
  - Values such as `1,00`, `5,00`, `0,00` appear as layer thickness or property indicators
- **Axis label (right side, vertical):** Graduated scale with units likely in **мГл** (milligals) or **нТл** (nanoTeslas); values range from approximately **−1400** at bottom to **+200** at top
- **Horizontal axis:** Distance in kilometers or meters, with markers at regular intervals (numbers partially legible: **0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7...** possibly in tens of km)
- **Reference line annotation (left):** `Лин А` or similar (Line A — baseline reference)
- **Point annotations:** Small numbers near the circle symbols (borehole or station identifiers), partially legible

## Research Value

This profile provides a critical **integrated geophysical and geological cross-section** across a significant portion of the survey area, revealing major subsurface structural variations including a pronounced **positive gravity/magnetic dome** (potentially indicating a dense basement high or intrusive body) and a deep **negative anomaly trough** (possibly reflecting a sedimentary basin, graben, or low-density intrusion) on the eastern segment of the line.
